project.name: agent_py
project.description: A FastAPI plus Vue multi-provider AI assistant platform that combines chat, multimodal attachment handling, image generation, batch jobs, storage, billing, and admin features.

layers.name: Entry and API Layer
layers.description: Application entry points, router registration, middleware, and HTTP-facing backend contracts.

layers.name: Application Core Layer
layers.description: Task orchestration, assistant workflows, prompt extraction, matching, scheduling, and shared runtime utilities.

layers.name: Integration Layer
layers.description: Provider adapters, storage implementations, model registry logic, and external service boundaries.

layers.name: Data and Domain Layer
layers.description: Database models, SQL schema, migrations, service objects, and request or response contracts.

layers.name: Frontend Layer
layers.description: Vue application shell, views, state stores, components, composables, and browser-side services.

layers.name: Tooling and Tests Layer
layers.description: Project manifests, container configuration, documentation, and automated tests that support development and operations.

tour.title: Start With The System Shape
tour.description: Read the project overview and then jump into the backend and frontend entry points. These files show that the product is a FastAPI backend paired with a Vue single-page app for AI chat and image generation.

tour.title: Trace The Backend HTTP Surface
tour.description: The route modules define the public product surface: generation, assistant chat, model discovery, file upload, history, auth, and admin features. This is the best place to understand what the frontend and external callers can ask the service to do.

tour.title: Understand Request Orchestration
tour.description: Assistant planning and image execution split into two flows: one decides what kind of task a request represents, and the other executes queued image work. Together they implement the core unified assistant and async generation pipeline.

tour.title: Follow Provider And Model Resolution
tour.description: Model selection is not hard-coded to one vendor. The registry fetches remote metadata, then provider adapters translate internal requests into specific external APIs such as OpenAI-compatible relays, Midjourney, Ideogram, and other vendors.

tour.title: Map Persistence And Business Logic
tour.description: User requests, generated images, credentials, billing, downloads, and notifications all flow through the database manager and service layer. This area is where product rules such as balances, check-ins, withdrawals, and retention cleanup live.

tour.title: Read The Frontend User Journey
tour.description: The Vue app is organized around views, Pinia stores, and a shared API client. Image studio, chat, user center, and admin screens all consume the backend routes through reactive stores and SSE-powered notifications.
